title: additionalDirectories widens the acceptEdits auto-approve scope, but a cwd-anchored deny Edit(scripts/**) does NOT match inside the additional directory (anchor no-op) -> W1 multi-root trap

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/s9d-extra/other/x.txt with the content 'v=1'. Use only the Write tool - if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result. Just make the tool call, no commentary.

Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/s9d-extra/scripts/x.txt with the content 'v=1'. Use only the Write tool - if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result. Just make the tool call, no commentary.

Use the Write tool to create a file at $CASE_DIR/scripts/x.txt with the content 'v=1'. Use only the Write tool - if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result. Just make the tool call, no commentary.

notes: S9 GAPS G5 / backlog B6-rest + F4. TRUST GATE: additionalDirectories entries are IGNORED in an untrusted workspace (stderr 'Ignoring 1 permissions.additionalDirectories entry ... this workspace has not been trusted', cf P7-c) — arrange.configDir trusted:true pins trust so the case is reproducible regardless of the developer's real trust state; evidenceMarker records that the ignore warning did NOT appear (evidenceFound=false expected). SCRATCH EXPLORATION (2026-07-05, v2.1.201, trusted in-repo scratch): write to <extra>/other/x.txt -> CREATED (additionalDirectories + acceptEdits auto-approves outside-cwd writes, docs permission-modes: acceptEdits covers working directory or additionalDirectories); write to <extra>/scripts/x.txt with deny Edit(scripts/**) -> CREATED (the unanchored rule is cwd-relative and does NOT match the additional directory tree = anchor no-op); write to <cwd>/scripts/x.txt -> BLOCKED (control: same rule works at its cwd anchor). Same probes in an UNTRUSTED scratch: both extra writes auto-denied as ask (permission_denials=['Write']) with the Ignoring warning = additionalDirectories never loaded. Probe 3 headless is structurally INCONCLUSIVE (hard deny does not populate permission_denials and does not remove the Write tool); sdk gives DENIED_HARD. Fix form for the extra root is d2 (deny Edit(~/s9d-extra/scripts/**)).
hypothesis: W1 (multi-repo-workspace.md) assumes deny Edit(scripts/**) protects scripts/ everywhere. Measured: unanchored Edit rules are cwd-anchored, so once additionalDirectories brings another root into the workspace (and acceptEdits auto-approves edits there), the scripts/ tree under that root is NOT protected by the cwd-relative rule — the deny silently protects only <cwd>/scripts. Multi-root workspaces need one anchored deny per root (d2).
